design_philosophy.core_loop: Select a themed level, navigate platforming challenges, defeat boss to acquire power suit, use suit abilities to access new areas and progress to next levels
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Balancing traversal difficulty against limited resources; choosing level order strategically to unlock necessary suit abilities for subsequent stages
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as NOVA, a cyborg operative on a covert government mission to dismantle a rogue weapons corporation by infiltrating and destroying their research facilities
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Strict adherence to Mega Man's stage-select formula with four collectible power suits that gate progression rather than permanently upgrade the player; forces replayability of stages with different suit capabilities rather than linear character growth

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High agency through stage selection order; player can choose which of four initial levels to tackle first, potentially easing difficulty by selecting optimal suit-acquisition sequence

progression.upgrade_system: Four collectible power suits acquired by defeating mid-level bosses: Newt Suit (wall/ceiling climbing), Wet Suit (swimming), Rocket Suit (flight), Patriot Suit (defensive capabilities). Suits unlock new traversal options and secret areas
progression.unlock_mechanism: Four optional levels can be completed in any order to unlock Area 5; Area 5 completion unlocks final level. Suit abilities directly gate access to certain platforming sections
replayability_hooks: Multiple stage-select order permutations create different difficulty curves
Four distinct power suits encourage replaying stages with different movement capabilities
Hidden sound test and developer credits suggest bonus content discovery

world_building_techniques: Stage theming tied to Delta Foundation facility departments (implied cyborg research divisions)
Intro text establishes geopolitical stakes: weapons company threatening to sell cyborg soldier to hostile governments, triggering covert government response
Year 2200 setting establishes futuristic military-industrial context
Power suit names reflect stage environments (Newt/Wet/Rocket suits thematically matched to level designs)

boss_design.philosophy: Each of four mid-level bosses represents facility department and grants associated power suit upon defeat; final boss represents ultimate threat (the cyborg soldier threat from plot). Boss variety through environmental theming and suit mechanics

faction_indicators: U.S. Department of Defense (player faction: authoritative, defensive)
Delta Foundation (antagonist faction: corporate weapons research, destabilizing threat)
Unnamed hostile governments (implied external threat justifying covert operation)
danger_signals: Weapons research company operating outside government oversight
Cyborg soldier technology capable of destabilizing global security
Threat of technology proliferation to hostile state actors
One-week ultimatum creating artificial scarcity and urgency
environmental_storytelling: Facility levels designed around research departments (thematic staging reflects technological specialization)
Boomerang weapon (persisting from first game) suggests low-tech origin or intentional design choice by government
Power suit acquisition through combat suggests Delta Foundation already possessed advanced combat technology
